notes: Score ticked up to 3.7. The SVG character avatars are genuinely delightful — great personality. Rate limit fix is solid. Main gaps remain: accessibility is still weak, mobile experience needs love, and there are some code quality nits. The office view is the star of the show.
fixed_since_last_review: Previous suggestions addressed — rate limit fix confirmed working

suggestions.title: No skip-to-content link or focus management
suggestions.description: Keyboard users have no way to skip the header. Tab order goes through theme toggle before reaching content. Add a skip-to-main link and ensure the tab toggle buttons have proper focus indicators. The Office view has pointer-events:none on most chars, which is fine, but the Dashboard tab has no visible focus rings on cards.

suggestions.title: Color contrast issues in light mode
suggestions.description: Several text elements use var(--text-muted) (#6b7280) on white/light backgrounds which barely passes WCAG AA for normal text. The activity timestamps and stat labels are particularly hard to read. Darken --text-muted to at least #555 in light mode.

suggestions.title: No loading/error state for activity log
suggestions.description: If activity.json fails to fetch, there's no error indicator — it just shows 'No activity yet…' which is misleading. The workers section has a skeleton loader but activity does not. Add error handling UI for both fetches.

suggestions.title: Polling both endpoints every 10 seconds is aggressive
suggestions.description: 10-second polling of two raw GitHub URLs is fine for a demo, but could be smarter. Consider: (1) exponential backoff when data hasn't changed, (2) comparing ETags or content hashes before triggering re-renders, (3) 30s interval when tab is not focused (use visibilitychange API).

suggestions.title: ActivityLog key prop could cause collisions
suggestions.description: Using `entry.timestamp + entry.worker` as key is fragile — multiple entries can share the same timestamp (e.g., the bulk hire at 19:21). Use index or generate unique IDs in activity.json. Currently works by luck.

suggestions.title: Office view characters overlap on narrow screens
suggestions.description: At ~600px width, the desk positions (percentage-based) cause character avatars to overlap each other. The CSS responsive breakpoints hide names at 480px but don't adjust desk layout. Consider reducing desk count or stacking vertically on mobile.

suggestions.title: Add a 'last seen' or clock-in time for workers
suggestions.description: Workers have updated_at but the dashboard doesn't show it. Users can't tell if a worker has been 'working' for 5 minutes or 5 hours. Show relative time like '⚡ Working · started 2h ago' on each card.

suggestions.title: DEFAULT_ROSTER duplicated between App.jsx and Office.jsx
suggestions.description: Office.jsx has a DEFAULT_ROSTER fallback, and App.jsx fetches roster from workers.json. If the fetch fails, Office uses its hardcoded fallback which could go stale. Extract to a shared constants file.

suggestions.title: Footer quote could rotate
suggestions.description: The footer always says 'If it compiles, ship it.' — would be fun to have a small array of Bloberto-isms that rotate. Tiny detail but adds personality. Could even pull from a quotes array in the data folder.

suggestions.title: No favicon or PWA manifest
suggestions.description: Currently using the default Vite SVG favicon. A custom Bloberto favicon (the melty blob face) would be a nice touch. Could also add a basic manifest.json for PWA install capability.
